evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: 12 study CI lines with inverse-variance-weighted squares, red diamond for DerSimonian-Laird pooled estimate, dashed null line at 0.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; PDF contains Type3 DejaVu Sans (confirmed via strings: '/Subtype /Type3'), which is the matplotlib default — text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for study squares and red (#d62728) for pooled diamond is conventional and colorblind-distinguishable; no green/red directional confusion, but red for pooled could imply negative directionality to some readers.
evidence.layout_density: 8×6 inch figure with 12 rows + pooled row; tight_layout used; y-axis labels include multi-line pooled label; no obvious overlap, but no explicit column for weights/CI values (common in forest plots).
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption is detailed and matches figure content; figure title is redundant with caption in a journal context; no numeric weight column or separate CI text column that reviewers often expect in forest plots.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (18 KB) but uses Type3 embedded fonts (not Type1/TrueType via fonttype=42); text is not selectable — script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (52 KB), figure.svg (51 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the visual encoding (squares sized by inverse-variance weight, horizontal lines = 95% CI, red diamond = pooled), gives specific numerical finding (pooled 0.246; 95% CI 0.121–0.371), and reports heterogeneity statistics (Q=62.01, τ²=0.037).

overall_strengths: Caption is publication-quality: encodes visual elements, reports the key numerical finding, and includes heterogeneity statistics.
All required output files are present and the DerSimonian-Laird computation is correctly implemented in the script.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, so the PDF embeds Type3 (non-selectable) DejaVu Sans — a standard rejection point for journal submission.
Forest plot lacks a numeric CI/weight column alongside the study labels, which is a near-universal reviewer expectation for this figure type.
